Requires certain 100 MW-plus data processing centers to pay state noneducational ad valorem taxes and sales/use tax on specified construction, power, backup generation, and battery purchases beginning 2027-01-01, subject to targeted-county gubernatorial authority. Extends the data processing center abatement sunset.

Defines a "large load data center customer" as a data processing center customer with expected total peak demand of 150 MW or greater on contiguous parcels. Directs PSC public-interest review of retail electric service contracts to consider recovery of incremental costs from the data center and positive benefits for other retail electric customers.

sponsors: Rep. Neil Rafferty, Rep. Leigh Hulsey, Rep. Allen Treadaway Tillman

status: (H) ENE, then CRA; status date 2026-01-20
key_provisions: Not a moratorium. Regulates "large energy use facilities" through RCA-approved utility contracts, cost-causation rules, long-term service commitments, direct assignment of facility-driven utility costs, and municipal community benefit agreements before contract approval. The introduced threshold is 20 MW peak electric demand or 20 percent of prior-year utility sales, and 2 billion standard cubic feet of gas annually or 20 percent of prior-year gas sales.

status: (S) L&C; status date 2026-04-22
key_provisions: Not a moratorium. Directly regulates data centers and utility service. The committee substitute requires data centers to pay facility-driven utility costs, provide financial security for utility capital investments above $1 million, use contracts that avoid fuel-supply and reliability risk shifting, include backup power and reliability planning, execute municipal CBAs, address waste heat, water use, broadband interconnection, digital equity, and file decommissioning and recycling plans. The committee substitute exempts data centers below 15 MW peak electric demand and 1.5 billion standard cubic feet of annual gas use.
